summary: High‑quality audience tape captures a stellar spring‑'73 show with smoking China Cat→Rider, Playin’ in the Band and a ripping Dark Star→Truckin’→Morning Dew.
review: The recording (a Betty Reel audience source) is clear and well‑balanced, letting listeners hear every instrument and even the crowd banter. Musically the night shines – Jerry’s guitar tone and solos are repeatedly praised, Phil’s deep‑zen bass underpins a tight rhythm section, and the band delivers classic jams from China Cat→Rider through Playin’ in the Band and a soaring Dark Star sequence.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most rating 4‑5 stars, uniformly praising both performance and sound quality; only one low star relates to streaming issues, not the show itself.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Reel source noted as clear, crisp and well‑balanced; no major dropouts or muddiness reported. Some users mention buffering when streaming but the file itself is praised as ‘perfect sounding’.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
The Promised Land > Bertha > Greatest Story
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening with the China‑Rider sandwich (including the Feelin’ Groovy jam), a first‑set climax on Playin’, and a second set that builds through The Promised Land → Bertha → Greatest Story into an epic Dark Star sequence. Transitions are praised as tight and energetic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone, wah‑wah work, and vocal deliveries (especially on Dew and Loser) receive constant praise; his solos are called ‘amazing’ and ‘mind‑melting.’
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is described as deep‑zen, supportive backbone of the jam; some fans love his playing but wish he’d stay out of vocals.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocal interplay (notably on China Cat→Rider) are highlighted as a ‘dialogue’ and a key part of the show’s energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards spar with Jerry, adding balance; his contributions in the jams are noted as essential.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and later Mickey) drumming is called driving, primal, and crucial to the power of Playin' and the Dark Star jam.
